constraints: Use one cohesive rogue-machine faction in the first level
Differentiate enemy silhouettes roles warning states and weight classes clearly
Prefer modular mechanical parts and reusable production patterns
Keep corporate mercenary biological and alien factions post-MVP
Use assembly testing storage security and industrial production zones
Connect the location directly to the machine faction technological loot and mission objective
Differentiate interior areas strongly enough to avoid repetitive factory corridors
Build a reusable modular industrial environment kit
Use a direct first-level mission premise
Allow intermediate production-system shutdown objectives
Do not require an extraction sequence prototype-launch story or complex branching finale
Keep the first climax modest and appropriate for level one
Differentiate the upgraded droid mainly through somewhat more advanced shooting
Do not require a giant multi-phase or factory-wide boss spectacle
Defer exact enemy and boss designs
Allow later broad inspiration from Robokill Red Storm and selected SAS 4 boss ideas
Create approximately four or five ordinary machine roles
Generate encounter variety through composition positioning and environment
Keep the roster small enough to animate balance telegraph and test thoroughly
Defer exact role and attack designs
Equip four weapons simultaneously
Include enough alternatives to test loadouts rewards shops and replay
Add further weapons only after the core arsenal proves fun
Defer exact weapon list families and balance values
Do not implement randomized item statistics stars augments or enchantments for the internal MVP
Evaluate the quality of the authored base weapons directly
Preserve a clean future extension path in data and save boundaries without implementing unused complexity
Award sealed strongboxes that resolve to the six-to-eight base weapons
Offer a small randomized weapon subset through a gameplay-currency shop
Validate the play earn open compare equip replay loop
Exclude advanced modifiers rerolls dismantling reservations elaborate tiers and mature economy systems
Use only the smallest real safe duplicate-handling solution needed
Render mechs enemies weapons and major machinery from offline 3D sources into 2D sprites
Use painted procedural or directly authored 2D for environments effects shadows overlays UI and finishing
Keep the runtime fully two-dimensional
Define shared perspective lighting palette scale outline texture and post-processing rules
Support future directional animation equipment variants damage states and cosmetics

open_questions_added: Committed human and AI-agent production team for the internal MVP
Exact first-level zone and checkpoint structure
Exact four-or-five enemy roles and upgraded-droid design
Minimal strongbox shop currency inventory and duplicate-handling rules
Offline 3D modeling rigging rendering and sprite-export toolchain
Audio music voice and localization production boundary
Stack and technical architecture after participation is known
Definition of done proof and testing requirements

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether the internal MVP is produced primarily by one human developer supported by AI agents and occasional outsourced specialist work, by a committed small human team with AI support, or as an early open or community-collaboration project. Preserve the one-level internal milestone, hybrid offline-3D and painted-2D pipeline, Windows PC first, fully offline campaign, future Android and co-op directions, and repository-first AI Assembly Line workflow. Include decision speed, workload, code and asset ownership, art audio and design coverage, review and integration overhead, parallelization, contributor onboarding, budget exposure, continuity risk, and implications for later stack architecture and task splitting. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Queue D-151 through D-160 and persist at D-160 unless the user changes the batching rule or requests a context handoff.
